content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

February 8, 1994

Paul M. Loya
Law Offices of Atkinson,
Andelson, Loya, Ruud & Romo
4450 Black Avenue
Suite A
Pleasanton, CA 94566

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-93-453

Dear Mr. Loya:

This is in response to your letter requesting advice on
behalf of Christine Sherrill, a member of the Board of Education
of the El Segundo Unified School District regarding her
responsibilities under the conflict-of-interest provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Since your advice request
does not refer to a specific governmental decision, we are
treating your request as one for informal assistance.²

QUESTIONS

1. What economic interests does Councilmember Sherrill have
by virtue of her position as a travel agent with the travel agency
"Where in the World Travel"?

2. May she participate in making school district decisions
that will have a financial effect on sources of income related to her?

3. Does Councilmember Sherrill have to disclose all sources of
income to her on her Statement of Economic Interests?

4. If a potential conflict of interest exists, how does
Councilmember Sherrill disqualify herself?

___________________

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations, Sections
18000-18954. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Section
83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Our File No. I-93-453
Page 2


                                    CONCLUSIONS

          1.    A public official who is a travel agent has an economic
     interest in (1) the airline, hotel, tour operator or other person
     who provided travel services or accommodations in the transaction;
     (2) the person who purchases or has a contract for travel services
     or accommodations through the agent; and (3) the person, travel
     agent, company, travel agency or other business entity for which
     the agent is an agent.  (Regulation 18704.3(c)(4).)

          2.    Councilmember Sherrill may not make, participate in
     making, or attempt to use her official position to influence a
     school district decision that will have a reasonably foreseeable
     material financial effect on any of the sources of income
     specified in Regulation 18704.3(c)(4), including the travel agency
     "Where in the World Travel."

          3.    Councilmember Sherrill is required to disclose all
     sources of income to her if the aggregate amount provided to,
     received by, or promised to, her from each source is $250 or more
     in a calendar year.  This would only apply if such sources of
     income are located, or doing business, in the jurisdiction of the
     city.

          4.    If Councilmember Sherrill is precluded from participating
     in making a governmental decision because of a conflict of
     interest, she must disclose the nature of her financial interest
     in the decision on the agency's official record.

                                      FACTS

          Christine Sherrill has been a member of the Board of
     Education of the El Segundo Unified School District since January
     1991.  In September 1992, she became affiliated with the travel
     agency, "Where in the World Travel" located in El Segundo.
     Councilmember Sherrill is an independent contractor for the travel
     agency.  She receives ten percent commission on all sales to her
     clients.  The clients pay the travel agency which, in turn, pays
     Councilmember Sherrill's commission.  Councilmember Sherrill pays
     five percent of her commission to the travel agency for office
     maintenance and reference material.  Councilmember Sherrill states
     that she has never been paid by the travel agency to do any work
     for the school district.

          The El Segundo Unified School District utilizes "Where in the
     World Travel" in making travel arrangements for school district
     personnel, including members of the Board of Education of the El
     Segundo Unified School District.  The school district used "Where
     in the World Travel" prior to Councilmember Sherrill becoming
     affiliated with the agency.  The school district makes travel
     arrangements with several employees of the travel agency and makes
     payments on those travel arrangements through purchase orders
     approved by the board of education.  Though the board approves
     travel arrangements, it has nothing to do with selecting the

Our File No. I-93-453
Page 3


travel agency used to make those arrangements. As a member of the
El Segundo Board of Education, Councilmember Sherrill has never
had the school district as a client at "Where in the World
Travel." The school district has never entered into any contract
for services with the travel agency.

ANALYSIS

Conflict of Interest:

Section 87100 prohibits public officials from making,
participating in, or using their official position to influence a
governmental decision in which they know or have reason to know
they have a financial interest.

A public official makes a governmental decision or
participates in the making of a governmental decision whenever the
public official votes on a matter, commits the agency to a course
of action, or enters into any contractual agreement on behalf of
the agency. (Regulation 18700(b).)

An official has a financial interest in a governmental
decision within the meaning of Section 87100 if it is reasonably
foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial
effect, distinguishable from its effect on the public generally,
on the official, or on a member of the official's immediate
family,3 or on:

* * *

Any source of income, other than gifts and
other than loans by a commercial lending
institution in the regular course of business on
terms available to the public without regard to
official status, aggregating two hundred fifty
dollars ($250) or more in value provided to,
received by or promised to the public official
within 12 months prior to the time when the
decision is made.

(Section 87103(c).)

Accordingly, a public official may not make, participate in
making, or attempt to use his or her official position to
influence a governmental decision if the decision will have a
reasonably foreseeable and material financial effect,
distinguishable from the effect on the public generally, on the
official, or on a member of the official's immediate family, or on
any of the official's economic interests specified in Section
87103, including a source of income.

3 An official's "immediate family" includes his or her spouse and
dependent children. (Section 82029.)

Our File No. I-93-453
Page 4


     For purposes of the Act, "commission income" means gross
payments received as a result of services rendered as a broker,
agent, or other salesperson for a specific sale or similar
transaction.  (Regulation 18704.3(b).)

     Generally, commission income is treated as income derived
from each of the participants in the transaction.  Regulation
18704.3 provides guidelines for the attribution of commission
income for conflict-of-interest purposes.  For example, in the
sale of an insurance policy resulting in a commission of $250 or
more, a public official who is an insurance broker or agent has an
economic interest in the insurance company providing the policy,
the person purchasing the policy and the brokerage firm, agency,
company or business entity through which the official conducts
business.  (Regulation 18704.3(c)(1).)

     Similarly, a public official who is a travel agent has an
economic interest in (1) the airline, hotel, tour operator or
other person who provided travel services or accommodations in the
transaction; (2) the person who purchases or has a contract for
travel services or accommodations through the agent; and (3) the
person, travel agent, company, travel agency or other business
entity for which the agent is an agent.  (Regulation
18704.3(c)(4).)

     Thus, Councilmember Sherrill may not make, participate in
making, or use her official position to influence a decision that
will have a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on
any of the persons or entities specified in Regulation
18704.3(c)(4), including the travel agency "Wherein the World
Travel," a source of income to her.

     The effects of a decision are reasonably foreseeable if there
is a substantial likelihood that they will occur.  To be foreseeable, the effects of a decision must be more than a mere
possibility; however, certainty is not required.  (Downey Cares v.
Downey Community Development Com. (1987) 196 Cal.App.3d 983,
989-991; Witt v. Morrow (1977) 70 Cal. App. 3d 817, 822; In re
Thorner (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 198.)  The Act seeks to prevent more
than actual conflicts of interest; it seeks to prevent even the
appearance of a possible conflict of interest.  (Witt v. Morrow,
supra, at 823.)

     Regulation 18702 sets forth the guidelines for determining
whether an official's economic interest in a decision is
"materially" affected as required by Section 87103.  If the
official's economic interest is directly involved in the decision,

Our File No. I-93-453
Page 5


then Regulation 18702.1 applies to determine materiality.⁴ If the
official's economic interest is not directly involved in the
decision but is indirectly affected by the decision, or if the
effect of the decision is not material under Section 18702.1, then
it must be determined if the effect is material under Regulations
18702.2 through 18702.6.⁵

Disqualification:

For purposes of determining whether disqualification is
required under the provisions of Sections 87100 and 87103(c), the
full gross value of any commission income for a specific sale or
similar transaction is attributed to each source of income in that
sale or transaction. (Regulation 18704.3(d).)


⁴ Regulation 18702.1(b) states:

    A person or business entity is directly involved in
    a decision before an official's agency when that
    person or entity, either personally or by an agent:

        (1) Initiates the proceeding in which the
    decision will be made by filing an application,
    claim, appeal, or similar request or;

        (2) Is a named party in, or is the subject
    of, the proceeding concerning the decision before
    the official or the official's agency.

        (3) A person or business entity is the
    subject of a proceeding if a decision involves the
    issuance, renewal, approval, denial or revocation
    of any license, permit, or other entitlement to, or
    contract with, the subject person or business
    entity.

⁵ The regulations are:

    18702.2. Material Financial Effect: Business Entity
Indirectly Involved in the Decision

    18702.3. Material Financial Effect: Ownership Interest in
Real Property Indirectly Involved in the Decision

    18702.4. Material Financial Effect: Leasehold Interest in
Real Property Indirectly Involved in the Decision

    18702.5. Material Financial Effect: Nonprofit Entity Source
of Income or Gifts Indirectly Involved in the Decision

    18702.6. Material Financial Effect: Source of Income or Gifts
Who is an Individual Indirectly Involved in the Decision

Our File No. I-93-453
Page 6

Since we can safely assume that the travel agency "Where in
the World Travell" is a source of income to Councilmember Sherrill
in the amount of $250 or more in a calendar year, she must
disqualify herself from participating in any governmental decision
that will have a material financial effect on "Where in the World
Travel." Clearly, the school district's decision to approve
travel arrangements will have a financial effect on the travel
agency; however, we do not have sufficient information to
determine whether those financial effects are material.

If the official is precluded from participating in making a
decision because of a conflict of interest, the official must
disclose the nature of his or her financial interest in the
decision on the agency's official record pursuant to Regulation
18730. (Regulation 18730(b)(5).) Thus, for example, if
applicable, Councilmember Sherrill must state on the record that
she is disqualified from participating in the school district's
decision to approve travel arrangements because the agency that
handles those arrangements is "Where in the World Travel," a
source of income to her.

Disclosure:

Section 87302 provides that designated employees must
disclose sources of income which are reportable. A source of
income is reportable if the income or source of income may
foreseeably be affected materially by any decision made or
participated in by the designated employee by virtue of his or her
position with the agency.

Thus, Councilmember Sherrill is required to disclose all
sources of income to her if the aggregate amount provided to,
received by, or promised to, her from each source is $250 or more
in a calendar year. With the exception of gifts, this would only
apply if such sources of income are located, or doing business, in
the jurisdiction of the city.

If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5901.⁶ I have
enclosed a copy of our conflict of interest brochure for your
convenient review.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

[signature]
By: Deanne Stone
Senior Commission Counsel

_______________
⁶ Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries. Alternatively, copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the commission at a
cost of 10¢ per page.
